jobs.title: Project Coordinator
jobs.description:
Company Description

KPFF Special Projects Division

Since 1996, KPFF's Special Projects Division has been at the forefront of addressing specialized challenges in project development. With over 150 team members in three offices strategically located in Seattle, WA, Tacoma, WA, and Long Beach, CA, our approach combines dedicated project managers, a skilled technical team, and a commitment to innovation. We offer a comprehensive range of services including civil, structural, and mechanical engineering, machinery design, construction and project management, and planning services.

We specialize in the management and design of heavy civil, structural, industrial, and waterfront infrastructure projects across the United States. Our team thrives on solving unique, multi-disciplinary challenges, collaborating closely with clients such as the Ports of Long Beach, Los Angeles, Tacoma, and Seattle, as well as various municipalities and gvernment agencies.

Join us at KPFF Special Projects Division and be part of a dynamic team driving innovation and excellence in project delivery.

Job Description

KPFF’s Special Projects Division in downtown Seattle, Washington, is seeking a full-time Project Coordinator with a minimum of five years of relevant experience to contribute to our engineering, project management, and construction management projects. In this role, you will support Principals, Project Managers, design teams, and construction management teams to help deliver projects within scope, schedule, and budget.

This position supports all phases of project delivery, from schematic design through construction and closeout, by providing project coordination, contract administration, project accounting, and administrative and business development support.

What You Will Be Doing

With minimal supervision and guidance, responsibilities may include:

Project Coordination

Assist with project controls, assisting in project scope, schedule, and budget monitoring
Set up new project files, maintain files throughout the project lifecycle, and archive closed projects
Develop and maintain project schedules and communicate key milestones
Schedule project meetings, prepare agendas and meeting notes, and track action item lists
Coordinate construction support activities, including shop drawing/submittal reviews and RFI routing
Coordinate project deliverables, QA/QC activities, and communication with clients and subconsultants
Prepare and/or finalize reports, memorandums, and technical documents
Act as the point-of-contact for project managers as needed

Contract Administration

Prepare, review, and oversee contracts, amendments, subconsultant agreements, and task orders
Track certificates of insurance and contractual compliance requirements
Assist with the creation and compilation of fee estimates and level of efforts
Finalize and execute Work Directives/Task Orders and perform contract administrative management

Project Accounting

Set up and manage spreadsheets for cost tracking and billing
Monitor contract expenditures, report variances, and assist in monthly invoicing
Prepare and review client invoices and progress reports
Respond to budget, invoice, and contract inquiries from KPFF staff, clients, and subconsultants

Group Administration

Develop and maintain MS Word and Excel templates to support business development and group operations
Support development of financial tracking tools and reports
Conduct research in active and potential market sectors
Support the development of promotional and business development materials

Qualifications

What we are looking for:

Effective communicator: verbal and written, tailored to collaborative project environments
Proactive thinker: anticipates the needs of project managers and design teams
Critical thinker: exercises independent decision-making and problem-solving
Continuous learner: approaches professional growth with humility and curiosity
Collaborative Team player: thrives in a collaborative, high-performing culture

Position Requirements

Minimum 5 years of experience in project coordination, project administration or a related role
Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite, SharePoint, MS Project, Bluebeam, and Smartsheet
Experience with accounting, invoicing, billing, and contract administration
Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple concurrent tasks and deadlines
Superior verbal and written communication skills
Ability to work independently while supporting multiple levels of professional staff.
Professional demeanor, positive attitude, and self-motivated work ethic.

Desired Qualifications

Experience in the AEC field / design-build / engineering environment
Experience with public-sector contracting and invoicing procedures
Experience with construction documents, including plans and specifications
Technical writing certification and/or experience

Additional Information

Physical Demands And Work Environment

Duties are primarily conducted in a standard office setting, requiring prolonged periods of sitting at a desk.
Occasionally may be required to lift objects weighing up to 15 lbs.
Occasionally visiting project locations and performing indoor/outdoor field observations, which may involve walking, standing, kneeling, climbing stairs/ladders and navigating uneven terrain.

Culture And Benefits

Join KPFF for a career adventure that celebrates your expertise and fuels your development. Here, autonomy and innovation converge, empowering you to tackle complex challenges and blossom into a consulting trailblazer. Our culture fosters teamwork, ownership, and common values, ensuring that your accomplishments are celebrated and your career trajectory is in your hands. At KPFF, we're not just coworkers; we're a vibrant community fueled by collaborative creativity, offering you the chance to leave a lasting mark on pioneering projects.

We are passionate about supporting our employees through flexible and comprehensive Benefits. Our Benefits include:

401(k) retirement savings plan with employer contribution (regardless of employee contribution)
Medical insurance (two plans available to choose from)
Dental insurance
Vision Insurance
Health Savings Account (HSA) with employer contribution
Healthcare and Dependent Care Flexible Spending Accounts (FSA)
Life insurance
KPFF Paid Family Leave
Short-term and Long-term disability insurance
Paid holidays (including two floating Holidays)
Paid time off (vacation, sick, jury duty)

Other Perks

Hybrid work environment
Professional development education opportunities
Mentorship program
Monthly social events
Professional Licensure Recognition Program
Winter parties and summer picnics
Office committee opportunities
Community projects participation

Compensation

The base salary for this role is competitive and will be determined based on the candidate's experience, education, and skills. The expected range for a qualified candidate is $35-$40 an hour. Additionally, we offer performance-based bonuses to recognize and reward outstanding contributions in each Reporting Center. During the interview process, we'll provide detailed information and address any questions you may have about the overall compensation package and benefits.

KPFF

KPFF, a multi-office, multi-discipline engineering firm, is committed to Excellence, Trust, Relationships, Stability, and Passion. With over 60 years of experience, we provide creative solutions for diverse projects, scales, and industries. Our nearly 1,500 professionals in 29 offices nationwide thrive in a decentralized structure, offering abundant flexibility and numerous opportunities for professional growth. At KPFF, join a legacy of excellence where every team member has the opportunity to contribute to innovative solutions and impactful projects.

KPFF Consulting Engineers participates in E-Verify and will provide the federal government with your Form I-9 information to confirm that you are authorized to work in the U.S.

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description:
About Us

Groundbreaker. Game changer. Pioneer.

TRC has long set the bar for clients who require more than just engineering, combining science with the latest technology to devise innovative solutions that stand the test of time. From pipelines to power plants, roadways to reservoirs, schoolyards to security solutions, clients look to TRC for breakthrough thinking backed by the innovative follow-through of an industry leader.

TRC's professionals work with a broad range of commercial, industrial and government clients and the communities they serve. We deliver breakthrough solutions that address local needs -- so our clients can better succeed in an ever-changing world.

Working at TRC means tackling interesting, meaningful projects. We pride ourselves on our collaborative spirit, entrepreneurial zeal and agile corporate structure. We recognize that the expertise of our staff is our strongest asset, so we generously reward employees for successful performance and invest in their careers through training and the development of new skills and certifications.

Overview

TRC is seeking a full-time Environmental Scientist in the Planning, Permitting, and Licensing practice of our East Providence, RI office to support field and office needs. The successful candidate will work under the supervision of Project Managers and Team Leaders and will integrate with the regional team to complete a variety of technical tasks and projects.

Responsibilities

Support variety of field programs including, but not limited to, wetlands delineations, vegetation surveys, vernal pool surveys, wildlife studies, and water quality monitoring.
Maintain and operate field monitoring and sampling equipment.
Collect multimedia samples (water, soil, etc.), assure acceptable sample handling, and coordinate with laboratories as needed.
Ensure that collected data are accurately documented, well-organized, and of high quality.
Prepare permit applications and participate in regulatory permitting processes for a variety of project types. This is expected to include permitting through municipal conservation commissions and boards, state agencies (e.g., MassDEP, CT DEEP, RIDEM), and related federal permitting (e.g., Sections 404, 10, 408).
Execute work in accordance with work plans/scopes of work.
Compile and analyze ecological and other environmental data.
Write reports and impact assessments.
Prepare permit applications and attend project-related meetings.
Perform physically demanding tasks in varying environments and conditions (i.e., heat, cold) including completing field sampling activities, lifting and carrying equipment, and operating in remote locations. The position requires significant field work (generally single-day events, though overnight travel may also be required).
Other duties and special projects as assigned.
Demonstrate a commitment to safety and adhere to TRC Health & Safety Standards in all field-related activities.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in biology, ecology, environmental science, forestry, wetland science, or related field.
Familiar with digital field technology (GPS units, tablets, data collection apps, etc.).
Five years of relevant professional experience.
Excellent written and verbal communications skills. Position requires ability to communicate effectively with external parties (e.g., clients, agency staff, stakeholders) as well as internal ones (e.g., project managers, task managers, field staff).
Prior permitting experience and familiarity with environmental regulatory programs in New England.
Previous experience leading field sampling efforts and conducting field work.
Self-motivated and detail oriented. Must have the ability to safely work through issues encountered during field activities.
Must have a valid driver's license and maintain a safe driving record. Must be able to pass a pre-employment motor vehicle records check and drive a company vehicle (truck).
Exhibit proficiency in data management and reporting using standard office productivity software (Microsoft Word, Excel, etc.).

Additional Valued Skills

Experience with small boats (outboard motorboats, Jon boats, canoes, kayaks). Holding a boating safety certificate is also desired but not required.
Current OSHA HAZWOPER 40-hr training or ability to obtain.

Benefits*: TRC offers a competitive benefit package consisting of:

Medical, dental, vision, and disability insurance.
401k package that includes both traditional and Roth IRA options and Company match.
Paid time off contingent upon full time or part time status and level of seniority (ranging from 15 to 25 days per year).
All full-time employees enjoy a minimum of 8 Paid Holidays per year.
TRC ensures that all employees, including those that work part-time, receive paid sick, family, and disability leave in accordance with the laws of their state of residence.
These benefits are applicable to US employees depending on their employment status (full time, part time, or temporary). TRC offers jurisdiction specific benefit packages for International and Puerto Rico employees.

Compensation: In accordance with local law, the below salary range is applicable for the job and location associated with this requisition. Compensation will vary based upon experience, education, skill level, geographical location, and other compensable factors.

Other Compensation: Employee may be eligible for participation in the Company’s annual Discretionary Bonus Program which is contingent on Company, Business Segment, and Individual performance against Key Performance Indicators which vary by year and employee.

Disclaimers

TRC is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. All employment decisions are made based on qualifications, merit, and business needs. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

The complete job description and application are available on TRC’s career site.

TRC accepts applications for this position on an ongoing, rolling basis and reserves the right to cancel this posting at any time.

Salary Range

USD $64,584.00 - USD $85,000.00 /Yr.

jobs.title: Geologist  - Exploration Project
jobs.description:
Northern Star Resources Limited (ASX: NST) is an Australian ASX 100 gold producer with three Tier-1 world-class projects located in highly prospective and low sovereign risk regions of Australia and North America. Northern Star is the largest Australian gold producer and among the top 10 gold miners globally. Northern Star continues to invest in building a strong asset base through strategic acquisitions and aggressive exploration to extend the mine lives across its world-class operations and generate earning accretive value for its Shareholders through operational effectiveness.

What do we offer?

- Vibrant hub of activity on site, with the ability to be part of successful and productive team 

- Permanent position and extended mine-life offering job security.

- Excellent remuneration package with bonuses, insurances, 401k and paid leave.

- Ongoing development opportunities and growth within a company that continuously invest in their people.

The Project Geologist Exploration reports to the Exploration Superintendent, and the primary role is to undertake planning, and supervision of exploration activities, interpretation and reporting of exploration results in a safe, effective and efficient manner, to produce increases in mineral resources.

SPECIFIC DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES

Safety Requirements

Display a clear commitment to safety and health and seek to eliminate or control the risks of injury and/or damage to equipment.

Operate in accordance with and comply with the Company’s Safety and Health Policy, and with all site policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.

Proactive involvement with continuous OHS improvement processes.

Active participation in all communication and awareness systems.

Actively participate in hazard reporting and other risk identification and control processes to mitigate risks in the workplace.

Follow exploration safety protocols when working around helicopters and performing helicopter-assisted fieldwork and drill operations.

Certification of bear safety shotgun course.

Environmental Requirements

Be aware of the environmental consequences associated with the tasks they perform and ensure environmental risks of tasks are assessed and mitigated prior to starting them.

Display a clear commitment to responsible environmental management.

Operate in accordance with the Company’s Environment Policy, and comply with other site environmental policies, procedures and regulatory requirements.

Manage all activities in a manner that prevents pollution and minimizes damage to the natural environment.

Minimize waste generation through reduced consumption and increased re-use and recycling.

Specific Departmental Requirements

Plan, budget, and supervise the execution of exploration programs to test targets, define or upgrade mineral resources.

Supervise and mentor Graduates, Geologists and Geological Technicians.

Supervise contractors and suppliers engaged in the exploration activities on the project area.

QAQC monitoring and validation of exploration results.

Provide timely and relevant information as requested by the supervising geologist.

Assist in the preparation of annual budgets and statutory reports.

Deliver periodic progress reports on time and in the correct format.

Liaise with Growth, Resource and Operational Geology functions, Metallurgists and Engineers.

Undertake additional responsibilities as reasonably requested by the supervisor.

Qualifications Required

Degree majoring in Geology from a recognized tertiary institution.

Minimum 3-5 years of relevant progressive experience.

Knowledge of mapping, structural geology, geochemistry, geophysics, drilling and sampling techniques. Some understanding of resource estimation methods and experience in field work.

Good communication skills.

Current Driver’s License from a U.S. State.

Desired Experience & Competencies

Able to manage a prospect scale project including, interpretation, drill design and drilling logistics.

Competent user of Leapfrog Geo, able to create a resource ready geological model.

Computer literacy – Microsoft Office, AcQuire, Reflex Hub, ArcGIS and LeapFrog Geo.

Demonstrate the ability to maintain or exceed best industry practice in all geological and environmental tasks.

Flexibility to adapt to improved procedures and practices.

First Aid certificate.

MSHA Certification

Why join us?

When you join our Company, you join the Northern Star Team. You will be engaged in a dynamic work environment where you will be valued as an integral part of the team, and where you can develop and grow with our business.

Northern Star actively supports and contributes to the local community in which we operate. We are a proud supporter of the Veterans' Employment Commitment and recognise the advantage of a diversified workplace including diversity of gender, thought, experience, and ethnicity. We encourage applications from diverse backgrounds and strive for workplace excellence.

Visa Sponsorship is not available for this position. 

Want to know more about Northern Star? Check out our website here: http://www.nsrltd.com/ or follow us on linked in: https://www.linkedin.com/company/northern-star-resources-limited/

Shortlisting for this role will begin immediately. 

Candidates living in the Fairbanks North Star Borough, Delta and Tok regions will be given first consideration.

Due to the large volume of applications, only applicants who are shortlisted will be contacted.

jobs.description:
Description

JOB DESCRIPTION

The primary responsibility of the Data Analyst will be in collecting, analyzing, and visualizing data to drive informed decision making in conjunction with the project controls team in support of the OPG service lines and program management. This position requires understanding of project management fundamentals, ensuring data integrity, developing reporting tools and templates, and providing actionable insights to improve cost, schedule, and project performance outcomes on a project and program level.

Work Location and Travel

Role will be in a hybrid environment, working both virtually and in office when required, Minimum 2 days per week in the Houston Office

Ability to travel domestically and internationally up to 10%

Responsibilities

Work Location and Travel

Role will be in a hybrid environment, working both virtually and in office when required, Minimum 2 days per week in the Houston Office
Ability to travel domestically and internationally up to 10%

Data Management, Analysis, and Reporting

Collect, validate, and maintain program data from multiple sources and systems (cost, schedule, financial, and project performance metrics)
Perform trend analysis, variance analysis, and forecasting for cost and project metric performance
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and completeness
Monitor data quality and implement controls to ensure accuracy and consistency
Analyze historical data to identify patterns and support predictive trends for project metric outcomes
Support initiatives to standardize and streamline data communications across multiple programs

Process Improvement

Develop and maintain standardized dashboards and reports using tools such as Excel and PowerBI
Recommend and implement improvement in standardization of metrics, data collection, and reporting
Assist in developing KPIs and metrics for program performance tracking
Investigate and support automation initiatives to streamline reporting and analytics

Stakeholder Support

Collaborate with project management, project controls, and program management to understand and support data needs and deliver visualized reporting to drive actionable insights
Provide ad hoc analysis and reporting to support service line management decisions
Partner with Program Managers to collect, organize, and visualize program performance data for external presentation
Participate in project or program reviews by providing accurate and timely data visualizations

Responsibilities

Supervisory Responsibilities

This position has no supervisory responsibilities.

Required

QUALIFICATIONS

Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Business, Engineering, or related field Three (3) years of experience in data analysis or related field, preferably within project controls, project management, or program management or, any combination of education and experience from which comparable knowledge, skills, and abilities have been achieved.
Proficiency in data visualization and analytics tools (Microsoft Power Apps, PowerBI, Excel)
Familiarity with project management and project controls principles
Ability to travel domestically and internationally up to 10%.

DESIRED

Offshore / oilfield operational experience
Familiarity with products and services offered by Oceaneering

We offer a comprehensive and competitive benefits package. Employee benefits vary by role, however, may include Health and Wellness, Mental Health, Retirement Savings, Life and Disability, Paid Maternity and Parental Leave, Paid Time Off, Tuition Reimbursement, and an Employee Assistance Program.

Oceaneering is a global provider of engineered services and products, primarily to the offshore energy industry. We develop products and services for use throughout the lifecycle of an offshore oilfield, from drilling to decommissioning. We operate the world's premier fleet of work class ROVs. Additionally, we are a leader in offshore oilfield maintenance services, umbilicals, subsea hardware, and tooling. We also use applied technology expertise to serve the defense, material handling, aerospace, science, and renewable energy industries.

jobs.title: HIM Data Analyst
jobs.description:
Site: The General Hospital Corporation

Mass General Brigham relies on a wide range of professionals, including doctors, nurses, business people, tech experts, researchers, and systems analysts to advance our mission. As a not-for-profit, we support patient care, research, teaching, and community service, striving to provide exceptional care. We believe that high-performing teams drive groundbreaking medical discoveries and invite all applicants to join us and experience what it means to be part of Mass General Brigham.

Job Summary

Data Analyst, Health Information Management (HIM)

Join a mission-driven health care team where accurate, meaningful data directly supports high-quality patient care, operational improvement, and better outcomes.

The Data Analyst, Health Information Management (HIM) will transform complex clinical, operational, and regulatory data into clear insights that help leaders, providers, and quality teams make informed decisions.

In this role, you will work with data from electronic health records, patient registries, administrative systems, and reporting platforms to identify trends, validate performance measures, support compliance requirements, and uncover opportunities to improve documentation, quality outcomes, and patient care delivery.

Why This Role Matters

This is an opportunity for a detail-oriented and collaborative analyst to make a measurable impact in health care. By turning data into insight, the Data Analyst, HIM will help advance quality, strengthen compliance, improve documentation, and support better outcomes for the patients and communities we serve.

Qualifications

Key Responsibilities

Gather, compile, validate, and analyze health information data from electronic health records, patient registries, administrative databases, and other internal sources.
Ensure data accuracy, completeness, integrity, and consistency by identifying discrepancies, resolving errors, and supporting quality assurance processes.
Apply data preparation, modeling, analysis, and visualization techniques to evaluate business, operational, clinical, quality, and population health performance.
Use statistical analysis tools and reporting software to assess patient outcomes, clinical trends, documentation patterns, and operational performance.
Develop recurring and ad-hoc reports, dashboards, and visualizations that monitor key performance indicators and support organizational goals.
Translate data findings into clear, actionable insights for leaders, physicians, department heads, quality improvement teams, and other stakeholders.
Collaborate with quality improvement and operational teams to identify opportunities to enhance patient care outcomes, improve workflows, and strengthen hospital processes.
Analyze clinical documentation and diagnosis coding to support improved diagnosis capture, risk adjustment, quality measure performance, and population health initiatives.
Support Uniform Data System (UDS), payer, quality measure, and regulatory reporting through data analysis, validation, and performance monitoring.
Maintain compliance with applicable reporting requirements, HIPAA standards, data privacy expectations, and organizational policies for protecting patient information.

Education and Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Health Information Management, health care analytics, public health, informatics, business analytics, or a related field required.
Two to three years of experience in health information management, health care data analysis, clinical analytics, quality reporting, or a related analytical role preferred.
Experience working with electronic health record data, clinical documentation, coding, quality measures, or regulatory reporting strongly preferred.

Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities

Knowledge of health care data standards, coding systems, data classification principles, and clinical documentation workflows.
Familiarity with electronic health record systems, health care data exchange standards, and data privacy regulations.
Working knowledge of SQL and data visualization tools such as Tableau or Power BI preferred.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex data sets and identify meaningful trends.
Excellent written, verbal, and presentation skills with the ability to communicate technical findings to clinical, operational, and administrative audiences.
High attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy, confidentiality, and quality assurance.
Ability to manage competing priorities, meet deadlines, and collaborate effectively across teams.

Additional Job Details (if Applicable)

Medical, Dental, and Vision insurance
Tuition Reimbursement
Generous Paid Time Off
50% Off MBTA passes
Access to childcare resources and emergency backup care
403(b), Cash Balance Retirement Plan, and Tax-Sheltered Annuity options
Exclusive “Perks” - enjoy discounts on tickets and passes for everything from ski resorts to museums to sporting events)

jobs.title: ProServe Solutions Engineer
jobs.description:
About SewerAI Corporation

SewerAI is transforming underground infrastructure management through AI-powered inspection and risk analysis. Our platform helps contractors, engineering firms, and utilities unlock valuable insights from sewer inspection data—turning hours of manual video review into actionable intelligence in minutes. After doubling our customer base over the past year, we’re now entering an exciting phase of accelerated growth.

About The Role

We're hiring a domain-first Professional Services Engineer who brings real sewer-inspection field expertise and wants to grow into technical delivery. As SewerAI scales rapidly, you'll own customer engagements end-to-end — from the first discovery call through validated delivery of a data migration or CMMS integration — becoming the trusted technical face for the customers you own.

This is not a traditional software-engineering role. You'll bring deep fluency in CCTV inspection, NASSCO standards, and inspection data quality, and you'll learn to use AI-assisted tooling to script, map, and transform data against the SewerAI API. Reporting into the ProServe team inside Engineering, you'll work directly with cities and utilities to solve the messy, real-world data problems that sit at the core of what we do.

Key Responsibilities

Customer Engagement & Delivery

Own the full engagement lifecycle — from technical discovery through scoping, ETL execution, and delivery — as the single accountable owner on each customer account.
Run discovery and intake calls with city operations and IT teams; produce Scoping Summaries that translate field realities into clear technical requirements.
Lead weekly customer check-ins, keeping engagements on track and resolving issues before they escalate.
Serve as the primary technical point of contact for your accounts, building trusted relationships with utility and contractor stakeholders.

Data Migration & Integration

Execute legacy data migrations into Pioneer, validating inspection data quality at each stage of the process.
Stand up and configure CMMS integrations (Cityworks, Cartegraph) against the SewerAI API.
Use AI-assisted tooling and the ProServe CLI/SDK to script, map, and transform customer data — with support from Engineering where needed.
Build reusable scripts and patterns over time that make the next engagement faster and more consistent.

Data Quality & Validation

Apply NASSCO coding standards (PACP/LACP/MACP) to validate inspection data submitted by customers prior to migration.
Identify and resolve data quality issues — coding errors, format mismatches, incomplete submittals — in collaboration with the customer and internal teams.
Maintain thorough documentation of engagement activity, data decisions, and delivery outcomes.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Partner with Technical Account Managers, Product, and Engineering to surface customer feedback, flag data edge cases, and continuously improve the ProServe delivery process.
Contribute to the development of ProServe playbooks, intake templates, and QA standards as the team scales.

Qualifications

No CS degree or software-engineering background required — this is a domain-first hire.
Hands-on field experience in sewer or wastewater inspection (CCTV, SSES, or similar programs).
Direct experience with inspection data and submittals — coding, QAQC, exports, or data management — for a contractor, utility, or engineering firm.
Fluency with NASSCO standards; PACP/LACP/MACP certification preferred, or the background to certify quickly.
Nice to have: prior contractor, utility, or engineering-consulting background; any exposure to scripting, SQL, or data tooling, even at a hobby level.

Skills & Competencies

Customer-facing confidence — comfortable running a discovery call with a city's operations or IT team and keeping it on track.
Strong ownership and follow-through; able to manage ambiguity and changing customer environments without losing momentum.
Practical communicator who translates technical topics into plain language for non-technical stakeholders.
Genuine curiosity about messy, real-world data problems and willingness to learn AI-assisted scripting, ETL work, and API tooling without an engineering background.
Exceptional attention to detail, particularly around data quality validation and NASSCO coding standards.

Compensation & Benefits

Base Salary: $90,000-120,000
Equity opportunities available

Benefits Include

Medical, Dental, Vision, Basic Life, 401(k), and more
Unlimited PTO
Tools and resources to support success
Competitive compensation with high-growth potential

Why Join SewerAI?

Join the fastest-growing team in wastewater tech and help modernize critical infrastructure with AI. You’ll own high-visibility projects, influence how our brand meets the market, and see your work drive measurable impact for customers and communities.

We are committed to providing a workplace free from discrimination or harassment on the basis of age, disability, sex, race, religion or belief, gender reassignment, marriage/civil partnership, pregnancy/maternity, or sexual orientation. All your information will be kept confidential according to EEO guidelines.

Compensation Range: $90K - $120K

is a leading specialty retailer of menswear, including suits, formalwear, sportswear, and a broad selection of business casual offerings. Our brands include Men's Wearhouse, Jos. A. Bank, Moores and K&G Fashion Superstore,
which offers merchandise for the entire family. We help people love the way they look and feel for their most important moments by delivering personalized products and services through our convenient network of stores and e-commerce sites.

About The Job

We currently have an exciting opportunity for a Sr Data Analyst – Store Analytics,
to join the Tailored Brands Enterprise Analytics team. The Sr Data Analyst – Store Analytics serves as a strategic analytics partner to Stores leadership and enterprise leaders, driving deeper understanding of store performance through diagnostic and exploratory analysis. This role goes beyond reporting to identify root causes of business performance, uncover opportunities, develop actionable recommendations, and influence decisions that improve sales, productivity, customer experience, and operational effectiveness.

The Sr Analyst independently leads complex analyses, develops scalable analytical frameworks, partners closely with business leaders and Data Engineering, and helps shape the evolution of the Store Insights capability.

What You’ll Do | Key Accountabilities

Lead complex diagnostic and ad hoc analyses that identify root causes of store performance, quantify business opportunities, and guide strategic decision-making across Stores.
Independently frame ambiguous business questions, formulate analytical approaches, synthesize multiple data sources, and translate findings into executive-ready recommendations.
Perform root cause analysis to explain sales trends, labor productivity, conversion, customer behavior, and operational performance, developing hypotheses and validating findings through quantitative analysis.
Proactively identify emerging risks and opportunities across the store fleet by monitoring leading performance indicators and conducting exploratory analyses.

Partner with Data Engineering and business stakeholders to validate semantic-layer logic, metric definitions, data quality, and reporting consistency.
Drive development of scalable Store Insights capabilities by identifying repeatable analytical opportunities, automating recurring analyses, and advancing self-service analytics.
Present findings and recommendations to senior leaders, influencing business strategy through clear storytelling and data-driven insights.

Develop repeatable analytics workflows that reduce manual reporting effort and improve the speed, quality, and consistency of insight delivery.
Create executive-ready charts, tables, dashboards, and written summaries that communicate findings clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Apply advanced analytics tools and techniques, including SQL, Power BI, Python, and external data sources, to solve complex business problems and continuously improve analytical capabilities.
Recommend new metrics, analytical approaches, and measurement frameworks that improve how Stores evaluate performance and make decisions.

What You’ll Bring | Skills & Experience

Bachelor’s degree in Analytics, Statistics, Economics, Mathematics, Business, Information Systems, or a related field; advanced degree a plus.
8+ years of progressive experience in analytics, business intelligence, consulting, retail analytics, or related quantitative disciplines, with demonstrated success solving complex business problems through data.
Demonstrated experience performing diagnostic, root cause, and exploratory analyses that influenced business decisions.
Proven ability to work independently with minimal direction, prioritize competing initiatives, and manage multiple high-impact analytical projects.
Experience presenting analytical findings to senior leadership and influencing cross-functional stakeholders.
Advanced SQL proficiency with experience optimizing complex queries and working with large enterprise datasets.
Strong experience with Power BI, semantic models, DAX, and modern BI best practices.
Working knowledge of Python, statistical analysis, and modern analytics workflows.
Experience applying statistical methods, experimentation, forecasting, or predictive techniques is preferred.
Experience in retail, multi-unit operations, consumer analytics, or store performance analytics strongly preferred.
Strong attention to detail and demonstrated experience with data validation, KPI governance, and metric consistency.
Ability to synthesize findings into clear written and verbal recommendations for business stakeholders.
Experience working with cross-functional partners to translate business questions into analytical solutions.

Please note that you do not need to qualify for all requirements to be considered. We encourage you to apply if you can meet most of the requirements and are comfortable opening a dialog to be considered.

Work Environment, Physical & Mental Demands

Ability to sit and work at a computer keyboard for extended periods of time
Ability to stoop, kneel, bend at the waist, and reach daily
Able to lift and move up to 25 pounds occasionally
Must utilize visual acuity, speech and hearing, hand and eye coordination and manual dexterity necessary to operate a computer and office equipment
Hours regularly 40 hours per week, as work dictates, from a remote office location

Benefits

This role is eligible for healthcare including medical, dental and vision, retirement savings (401k with a company match), income protection programs such as life, accident and disability insurance, paid time off for sick leave, vacation, bereavement, jury duty, and holidays, wellbeing program, commuter, adoption assistance, education assistance, legal services, and employee merchandise discounts. Work-Life Balance

At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good.

Your impact

When it comes to the most challenging environmental issues facing our communities today, we’re focused on developing and delivering the most innovative solutions to improve our tomorrow. We’re looking for an environmental professional (engineer or geologist) to continue their career at Jacobs – where we’ll empower you to learn and grow in the delivery of environmental site characterization and remediation projects for government and global private-sector clients – to make our world better.

Based out of Charlotte, NC, you’ll work with other scientists, engineers, subject matter experts, and project managers on exciting projects in which you will learn from others and pass along your knowledge. Responsibilities may include designing, performing, or overseeing soil, soil vapor, groundwater, and surface water investigations, building teams of colleagues and subcontractors, coordinating and managing fieldwork logistics, data analysis and interpretation, technical report writing, and participating in meetings with clients and regulators.

Please Note — This is a Hybrid Work-From-Home position. To ensure you’ll be successful in this role, we’re seeking someone local to the Charlotte, NC as it will require occasional field work and office visits.

Safety will be your priority, as you contribute to operational excellence and maintain the highest standards of Health, Safety and Environmental Compliance in everything you do.

Here's what you'll need

Here's What You'll Need

Bachelor’s degree in Geology or Hydrogeology or a related field.
A Fundamental of Geology (FG) certification or the ability to obtain one 12 months after hire.
Ability to perform fieldwork and obtain/maintain 40-hr HAZWOPER certification.
0-2 years of experience within environmental consulting or related field.
Ability to travel up to 60% - 75% time may be required and travel is not limited to the Charlotte, NC or Cary, NC vicinities.
Ability to build relationships, resolve conflict and participate in diverse teams.
Knowledge of computer software including but not limited to Microsoft Office, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint and data management software.
Local (or willing to relocate) to Charlotte, North Carolina.
United States Citizenship required
Valid Drivers license required

Ideally, you’ll also have

Master’s degree in engineering, geology, or other closely related technical field.
Professional Engineer or Geologist (P.E. or P.G.) license or ability to obtain and maintain.
Knowledge of USEPA regulations including CERCLA and RCRA.

Our health and welfare benefits are designed to invest in you, and in the things you care about. Your health. Your well-being. Your security. Your future. Employees have access to medical, dental, vision, and basic life insurance, a 401(k) plan, and the ability to purchase company stock at a discount. Eligible employees may also enroll in a deferred compensation plan or the Executive Deferral Plan. Jacobs has an unlimited U.S. Personalized Paid Time Off (PPTO) policy for full-time salaried/exempt employees, seven paid holidays, and caregiver leave. And certain roles may be eligible for additional rewards, including merit increases, performance discretionary bonus, and stock.

The base salary range for this position is $58,656.00 to $73,000.00. Within the range, individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or protected veteran status. Job posted on June 26, 2026. This position will be open for at least 3 days.

We value collaboration and believe that in-person interactions are crucial for both our culture and client delivery. We empower employees with our hybrid working policy, allowing them to split their work week between Jacobs offices/projects and remote locations enabling them to deliver their best work.

Your application experience is important to us, and we’re keen to adapt to make every interaction even better. Benefit from mentorship opportunities to help you grow your career at Jacobs and eventually begin to mentor others

Experience working on project sites and overseeing subcontractors

jobs.title: Clinical Research Data Specialist II
jobs.description:
The University of Southern California (USC), founded in 1880, is the largest private employer in the City of Los Angeles. As an employee of USC, you will be a part of a world-class research university and a member of the "Trojan Family," which is comprised of the faculty, students and staff that make the university what it is.

We are looking for a highly motivated and qualified candidate with experience in clinical trials data management.

Specific Job Duties

Coordinates and manages the clinical data for various phases of clinical study. Ensures data is documented and recorded as appropriate. Reads and understands clinical data from medical records and assists in obtaining outside documents. Extracts and enters required clinical data from medical records and patient research charts/reports into Clinical Research Forms (eCRFs/CRFs). Notifies PI or Study Coordinator of issues or violations.
Acts as primary site contact with sponsor’s Clinical Research Associate (CRA) for externally sponsored trials. Provides timely data entry, plans and organizes monitoring visits and responds to inquiries. Addresses all queries during and after audit for resolution.
Maintains currency of federal regulations governing the protection of human subjects such as Food and Drug Administration (FDA), Good Clinical Practice/International Conference on Harmonisation (GCP/ICH) guidelines, Office of Human Research Protections (OHRP), Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA), rules concerning reporting of Serious Adverse Events (SAEs) and violations with department training and feedback.
Interacts closely with quality assurance teams to ensure data accuracy on Clinical Research Forms (eCRFs/CRFs) prior to submission for in-house, National Cancer Institute (NCI) sponsored and Cooperative Group studies. Discerns basic data discrepancies/protocol violations.
Performs follow up for study patient survival by reviewing medical records. Contacts other institutions for data on patients hospitalized at other institutions.
Assists with study specimen procurement and handling.
Participates and attends internal and external new protocol start-up orientations and completes required protocol specific training.
Prepares and participates in audits of assigned studies such as National Cancer Institute (NCI), Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and pharmaceutical audits.
Participates in Quality Assurance Monitoring Committee (QAMC) in-house audits by reviewing assigned patient chart and reporting on findings as relates to protocol/patient compliance.
Completes spreadsheets for industry studies in real time to ensure sponsor is invoiced appropriately and timely.
Ensures confidentiality, accuracy, security and appropriate access of all data and records.
Performs other related duties as assigned or requested.

The hourly rate range for this position is $34.38 - $38.46
. When extending an offer of employment, the University of Southern California considers factors such as (but not limited to) the scope and responsibilities of the position, the candidate's work experience, education/training, key skills, internal peer equity, federal, state and local laws, contractual stipulations, grant funding, as well as external market and organizational considerations.

Minimum Education: Bachelor's degree, Combined experience/education as substitute for minimum education Minimum Experience: 1 year Minimum Field of Expertise: Experience in clinical trials data management. Requires strong attention to detail with prior data entry experience. Basic medical knowledge such as understanding medical terms and familiarity with various assessment criteria. Strong verbal and written communication skills. Able to manage time efficiently.

jobs.title: Metadata Analyst - 17052
jobs.description:
Position Title

Metadata Management Analyst (Data Governance & Metadata Management)

Location:

Vienna, VA
(Hybrid)

Position Status

Contract

Pay Rate

$60/HR ON W2

Position Description

We are seeking a highly analytical and detail-oriented Metadata Management Analyst
to support enterprise-wide Data Governance
, Metadata Management
, and Data Quality
initiatives within a large financial services environment.

This role will play a critical part in advancing data governance capabilities by partnering with business and technical stakeholders to establish, maintain, and improve metadata standards, business glossaries, data catalogs, data lineage, and governance processes. The ideal candidate will have experience working with data governance frameworks, metadata tools, and cross-functional teams to improve data transparency, integrity, and accessibility.

This is an excellent opportunity for a data governance professional who is passionate about helping organizations become more data-driven while ensuring strong governance, compliance, and data management practices.

Key Responsibilities

Partner with Business Data Owners, Data Stewards, and technical teams to identify, document, and catalog enterprise data assets.
Support the implementation and adoption of metadata management and data governance best practices.
Maintain and enhance metadata repositories, business glossaries, data dictionaries, and data catalog content.
Collaborate with stakeholders to establish consistent business definitions, terminology, and data standards across the organization.
Gather, analyze, and document business and technical requirements related to metadata, data lineage, and governance initiatives.
Support metadata governance processes for new projects, system enhancements, and enterprise data initiatives.
Identify metadata gaps, inconsistencies, and quality issues, and recommend corrective actions.
Assist with data lineage documentation and visualization to improve transparency across data flows and business processes.
Partner with technology teams to support metadata management tools, including testing, implementation, upgrades, and production support activities.
Support metadata classification, taxonomy development, and data lifecycle management efforts.
Maintain governance controls to ensure data is accurate, discoverable, and aligned with enterprise standards.
Facilitate stakeholder communication and promote data literacy across the organization.
Contribute to continuous improvement efforts related to data governance, metadata management, and enterprise data strategy.

Required Skills/Education

Required Qualifications:

Bachelor's degree in Information Systems, Data Management, Business, Computer Science, or a related field, or equivalent work experience.
Experience supporting Data Governance, Metadata Management, or Enterprise Data Management programs.
Experience working within financial services, banking, lending, or other large enterprise environments.
Hands-on experience with data catalogs, metadata repositories, data dictionaries, and business glossaries.
Understanding of data lineage, data lifecycle management, and enterprise data governance frameworks.
Experience documenting data assets, business definitions, metadata standards, and governance requirements.
Ability to collaborate effectively with both business and technical stakeholders.
Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Strong attention to detail with a focus on data accuracy and integrity.

Desired Qualifications

Experience supporting Enterprise Data Governance (EDG) programs.
Experience with metadata management platforms such as Alation, Collibra, Informatica, or similar tools.
Experience developing and maintaining business glossaries and enterprise data standards.
Knowledge of data classification frameworks, metadata taxonomies, and data governance policies.
Experience documenting and visualizing data lineage across enterprise systems.
Familiarity with data governance tool implementation, testing, and enhancement activities.
Exposure to lending data, financial services data domains, or regulatory reporting environments.
Understanding of enterprise data quality, risk management, and compliance practices.

About Seneca Resources

At Seneca Resources, we are more than just a staffing and consulting firm—we are a trusted career partner. With offices across the U.S. and clients ranging from Fortune 500 companies to government organizations, we provide opportunities that help professionals grow their careers while making an impact.

When you work with Seneca, you're choosing a company that invests in your success, celebrates your achievements, and connects you to meaningful work with leading organizations nationwide. We take the time to understand your goals and match you with roles that align with your skills and career path.

Our consultants and contractors enjoy competitive pay, comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage, 401(k) retirement plans, and the support of a dedicated team that advocates for you every step of the way.

jobs.title: Construction Project Coordinator (Assistant Project Manager)
jobs.description:
Location: Falls Church, VA

Job Type: Direct Hire

Starting Salary: From $73,000/year (higher compensation available based on experience)

About The Role

We are seeking a motivated Construction Project Coordinator
to support the successful planning, execution, and completion of commercial and institutional construction projects. This position is ideal for someone with experience in project management, estimating, scheduling, and subcontractor coordination who is looking to grow within a collaborative construction environment.

The successful candidate will assist with managing projects from pre-construction through closeout while ensuring schedules, budgets, safety standards, and quality expectations are met.

Position Overview

This role manages smaller construction projects valued up to approximately $4 million
or supports larger, more complex projects under the direction of senior project management. Projects may include commercial renovations, institutional facilities, design-build work, task order projects, and fast-track construction.

The ideal candidate understands the full project lifecycle—from planning and estimating through construction and closeout—and is comfortable working with a variety of project delivery methods, including design-build, IDIQ, lump sum, and fast-track projects. While much of the work is coordinated through subcontractors, experience managing self-performed work is highly valued.

Minimum Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or a related field with 2-3 years of experience working for a commercial or industrial general contractor, or
At least 5 years of directly related construction project experience without a degree
Experience coordinating both self-performed construction work and subcontracted trades
Understanding of commercial and institutional building renovation, including related site work
Proficiency with Microsoft Office applications, spreadsheets, email, and construction management software
Ability to safely lift up to 50 pounds
Ability to work in active construction environments, including walking job sites, climbing ladders and stairs, and performing frequent site inspections
Valid driver's license with a clean driving record
OSHA 30, First Aid, and CPR certifications
Current CQC certification
Engineer in Training (EIT) or Associate Constructor (AC) credential preferred

Candidates who do not currently hold the required certifications may be eligible for a grace period of up to six months to obtain them.

Key Responsibilities

Track and maintain project documentation, including RFIs, submittals, shop drawings, change orders, schedules, quality control records, safety documentation, and project closeout activities
Review construction documents and coordinate with technical team members to process shop drawings and other required project submissions
Assist with change management by maintaining logs, obtaining pricing from vendors, preparing estimates, and developing change order documentation
Support estimating efforts by performing quantity takeoffs using digital takeoff software, 3D modeling tools, and traditional methods
Develop, update, and monitor project schedules using industry-standard scheduling software
Coordinate project layouts by reviewing drawings, utilizing CAD information, and ensuring proper fit and alignment of project components
Assist with negotiating contracts, subcontracts, and purchase orders
Generate project reports using construction management and workflow software
Identify potential project risks and work proactively with the team to develop solutions
Help ensure compliance with project specifications, contract requirements, safety regulations, and applicable codes
Coordinate effectively with internal departments, including Accounting, Human Resources, and Information Technology
Support project closeout activities to ensure timely completion
Participate in constructability reviews and value engineering efforts to improve project efficiency and reduce costs
Attend team meetings, training opportunities, and professional networking events
Travel to project sites within the assigned service area as needed

What You'll Bring

Working knowledge of civil, architectural, mechanical, or electrical construction systems
Strong organizational, analytical, and problem-solving skills
Experience using estimating, scheduling, and reporting software
Excellent communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively with project teams, subcontractors, vendors, and stakeholders
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced construction environment

These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. jobs.title: Program Coordinator - Research Office LaunchLab
jobs.description:
The LaunchLab at West Virginia University Research Office is currently accepting applications for a Program Coordinator.

About The Opportunity

The Program Coordinator of the Morris L. Hayhurst LaunchLab supports daily operations and participant engagement for WVU’s primary entry point for undergraduate entrepreneurs. This position conducts student intake, supports outreach, and connects participants to the right resources across the WVU Innovation and Entrepreneurship Ecosystem. The Program Coordinator reports directly to the Director of the Innovation and Entrepreneurship Ecosystem.

Benefits

We strongly believe in work-life balance and keeping time for things we love outside our work. WVU offers generous benefits, including:

37.5-hour work week
13 paid holidays (staff holiday calendar)
24 annual leave (vacation) days per year (employee leave)
18 sick days per year (for when you’re ill, for when you need time to care for sick family, for your own, or your family’s, regularly scheduled medical appointments. Who is family for the purpose of this leave? A lot of people in your life including immediate relatives and in-laws as well as others considered to be members of your household living under the same roof)
WVU offers a range of health insurance and other benefits
401(a) retirement savings with 6% employee contribution match, eligibility to continue health insurance, and other retiree perks. Looking for more retirement benefits information? Check out retirement health insurance benefits, retirement income, and FAQ’s.
Wellness programs

What You'll Do

Intake and Student Support

Serve as a primary coach for LaunchLab participants across idea development, customer discovery, and venture readiness.
Conduct intake meetings and first-contact sessions with students entering the ecosystem.
Guide students through referral processes to relevant units within the ecosystem.
Support students in identifying next steps and connect them to relevant ecosystem resources.
Track student progress and flag participants who are ready to advance in the pipeline.
Support logistics for pre-accelerator cohort cycles, including scheduling, participant communication, and session setup.

Outreach and Ecosystem Engagement

Lead recruitment and outreach to undergraduate students across WVU colleges and disciplines.
Represent the LaunchLab at campus events, orientation sessions, classroom visits, and partner programs.
Build and maintain relationships with student organizations, faculty, and advisors who can serve as referral sources.
Support ecosystem-wide events including pitch competitions, workshops, and networking programs.
Collaborate with communications team on marketing plans for the LaunchLab.

Operations, Data, and Reporting

Oversee day-to-day operations of the LaunchLab space and student experience.
Manage student worker assignments, timesheets, and project oversight.
Maintain participant records and track engagement data.
Contribute to annual reporting, outcome tracking, and impact metrics for the LaunchLab.
Support budget monitoring and resource coordination as directed by the Director.

Other Duties As Assigned

Represent the LaunchLab and the ecosystem at institutional and community events.
Support special projects and initiatives as directed by the Director.

Bachelor's degree preferred in entrepreneurship, business, education, communications, or a related field or combination of education and experience.
A minimum of two (2) years of experience in the following:
Professional experience in program coordination, higher education, student services, or a related field.

Any equivalent combination of related education and/or experience will be considered.
All qualifications must be met by the time of employment.

Knowledge, Skills, And Abilities

Demonstrated ability to coordinate programs or events, including scheduling, participant communication, and logistics management.
Strong coaching and advising skills with experience working with students, early-stage entrepreneurs, or comparable populations.
Critical thinking skills and the ability to assess complex situations and develop practical solutions independently.
Strong oral and written communication skills, including experience with group presentations and one-on-one advising.
Ability to work across multiple departments and build effective collaborative relationships.
Comfort operating in an environment where goals and programs evolve over time.
Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.
Ability to maintain professionalism and composure during high-volume, high-pressure periods.
Ability to engage and support diverse groups of students in a welcoming and professional manner.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience building or managing programs with defined outcomes is preferred.
Experience working directly with student populations in a coaching, advising, or instructional role is strongly preferred.
Experience in a university innovation or entrepreneurship setting is a plus but not required.
Familiarity with entrepreneurship frameworks such as customer discovery, Lean Startup, and the Business Model Canvas is preferred.
Valid Passport (preferred for potential conference or partnership travel).

Requirements

Valid driver's license

About WVU

West Virginia University is a place of purpose and community. We take pride in our profound impact on the state of West Virginia and are committed to the personal and professional growth of our employees.

From the groundbreaking R1 research at our flagship campus in Morgantown to the career-oriented programs at WVU Potomac State in Keyser, and the technology-intensive programs at WVU Tech in Beckley, the contributions of WVU employees resonate across the state, touching lives and shaping futures.

At WVU, you will discover a supportive community that champions work-life balance and fosters a collaborative atmosphere. Our core values — service, curiosity, respect, accountability and appreciation — unite us as Mountaineers.

Join us at West Virginia University, where your work will make a lasting impact. To learn more about WVU, visit wvu.edu .

West Virginia University is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We invite all qualified applicants regardless of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, genetic information, or Veteran status.

new_jobs.title: Project Coordinator
new_jobs.description:
Company Description

KPFF Special Projects Division

Since 1996, KPFF's Special Projects Division has been at the forefront of addressing specialized challenges in project development. With over 150 team members in three offices strategically located in Seattle, WA, Tacoma, WA, and Long Beach, CA, our approach combines dedicated project managers, a skilled technical team, and a commitment to innovation. We offer a comprehensive range of services including civil, structural, and mechanical engineering, machinery design, construction and project management, and planning services.

We specialize in the management and design of heavy civil, structural, industrial, and waterfront infrastructure projects across the United States. Our team thrives on solving unique, multi-disciplinary challenges, collaborating closely with clients such as the Ports of Long Beach, Los Angeles, Tacoma, and Seattle, as well as various municipalities and gvernment agencies.

Join us at KPFF Special Projects Division and be part of a dynamic team driving innovation and excellence in project delivery.

Job Description

KPFF’s Special Projects Division in downtown Seattle, Washington, is seeking a full-time Project Coordinator with a minimum of five years of relevant experience to contribute to our engineering, project management, and construction management projects. In this role, you will support Principals, Project Managers, design teams, and construction management teams to help deliver projects within scope, schedule, and budget.

Project Coordination

Assist with project controls, assisting in project scope, schedule, and budget monitoring

Project Accounting

Set up and manage spreadsheets for cost tracking and billing
Monitor contract expenditures, report variances, and assist in monthly invoicing
Prepare and review client invoices and progress reports

Group Administration

Support development of financial tracking tools and reports
Conduct research in active and potential market sectors
Support the development of promotional and business development materials

Qualifications

Proactive thinker: anticipates the needs of project managers and design teams
Critical thinker: exercises independent decision-making and problem-solving
Continuous learner: approaches professional growth with humility and curiosity
Collaborative Team player: thrives in a collaborative, high-performing culture

Position Requirements

Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite, SharePoint, MS Project, Bluebeam, and Smartsheet
Experience with accounting, invoicing, billing, and contract administration
Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple concurrent tasks and deadlines
Superior verbal and written communication skills

Professional demeanor, positive attitude, and self-motivated work ethic.

Experience with public-sector contracting and invoicing procedures

Occasionally may be required to lift objects weighing up to 15 lbs.

new_jobs.description:
About Us

Groundbreaker. Game changer. Pioneer.

TRC has long set the bar for clients who require more than just engineering, combining science with the latest technology to devise innovative solutions that stand the test of time. From pipelines to power plants, roadways to reservoirs, schoolyards to security solutions, clients look to TRC for breakthrough thinking backed by the innovative follow-through of an industry leader.

TRC's professionals work with a broad range of commercial, industrial and government clients and the communities they serve. We deliver breakthrough solutions that address local needs -- so our clients can better succeed in an ever-changing world.

Working at TRC means tackling interesting, meaningful projects. We pride ourselves on our collaborative spirit, entrepreneurial zeal and agile corporate structure. We recognize that the expertise of our staff is our strongest asset, so we generously reward employees for successful performance and invest in their careers through training and the development of new skills and certifications.

Overview

TRC is seeking a full-time Environmental Scientist in the Planning, Permitting, and Licensing practice of our East Providence, RI office to support field and office needs. The successful candidate will work under the supervision of Project Managers and Team Leaders and will integrate with the regional team to complete a variety of technical tasks and projects.

Responsibilities

Support variety of field programs including, but not limited to, wetlands delineations, vegetation surveys, vernal pool surveys, wildlife studies, and water quality monitoring.
Maintain and operate field monitoring and sampling equipment.
Collect multimedia samples (water, soil, etc.), assure acceptable sample handling, and coordinate with laboratories as needed.
Ensure that collected data are accurately documented, well-organized, and of high quality.
Prepare permit applications and participate in regulatory permitting processes for a variety of project types. This is expected to include permitting through municipal conservation commissions and boards, state agencies (e.g., MassDEP, CT DEEP, RIDEM), and related federal permitting (e.g., Sections 404, 10, 408).
Execute work in accordance with work plans/scopes of work.
Compile and analyze ecological and other environmental data.
Write reports and impact assessments.
Prepare permit applications and attend project-related meetings.
Perform physically demanding tasks in varying environments and conditions (i.e., heat, cold) including completing field sampling activities, lifting and carrying equipment, and operating in remote locations. The position requires significant field work (generally single-day events, though overnight travel may also be required).
Other duties and special projects as assigned.
Demonstrate a commitment to safety and adhere to TRC Health & Safety Standards in all field-related activities.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in biology, ecology, environmental science, forestry, wetland science, or related field.
Familiar with digital field technology (GPS units, tablets, data collection apps, etc.).
Five years of relevant professional experience.
Excellent written and verbal communications skills. Position requires ability to communicate effectively with external parties (e.g., clients, agency staff, stakeholders) as well as internal ones (e.g., project managers, task managers, field staff).
Prior permitting experience and familiarity with environmental regulatory programs in New England.
Previous experience leading field sampling efforts and conducting field work.
Self-motivated and detail oriented. Must have the ability to safely work through issues encountered during field activities.
Must have a valid driver's license and maintain a safe driving record. Must be able to pass a pre-employment motor vehicle records check and drive a company vehicle (truck).
Exhibit proficiency in data management and reporting using standard office productivity software (Microsoft Word, Excel, etc.).

Additional Valued Skills

Experience with small boats (outboard motorboats, Jon boats, canoes, kayaks). Holding a boating safety certificate is also desired but not required.
Current OSHA HAZWOPER 40-hr training or ability to obtain.

Benefits*: TRC offers a competitive benefit package consisting of:

Medical, dental, vision, and disability insurance.
401k package that includes both traditional and Roth IRA options and Company match.
Paid time off contingent upon full time or part time status and level of seniority (ranging from 15 to 25 days per year).
All full-time employees enjoy a minimum of 8 Paid Holidays per year.
TRC ensures that all employees, including those that work part-time, receive paid sick, family, and disability leave in accordance with the laws of their state of residence.
These benefits are applicable to US employees depending on their employment status (full time, part time, or temporary). TRC offers jurisdiction specific benefit packages for International and Puerto Rico employees.

Compensation: In accordance with local law, the below salary range is applicable for the job and location associated with this requisition. Compensation will vary based upon experience, education, skill level, geographical location, and other compensable factors.

Other Compensation: Employee may be eligible for participation in the Company’s annual Discretionary Bonus Program which is contingent on Company, Business Segment, and Individual performance against Key Performance Indicators which vary by year and employee.

All employment decisions are made based on qualifications, merit, and business needs. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Northern Star continues to invest in building a strong asset base through strategic acquisitions and aggressive exploration to extend the mine lives across its world-class operations and generate earning accretive value for its Shareholders through operational effectiveness.

What do we offer?

- Vibrant hub of activity on site, with the ability to be part of successful and productive team 

- Permanent position and extended mine-life offering job security.

- Excellent remuneration package with bonuses, insurances, 401k and paid leave.

- Ongoing development opportunities and growth within a company that continuously invest in their people.

The Project Geologist Exploration reports to the Exploration Superintendent, and the primary role is to undertake planning, and supervision of exploration activities, interpretation and reporting of exploration results in a safe, effective and efficient manner, to produce increases in mineral resources.

SPECIFIC DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES

Safety Requirements

Display a clear commitment to safety and health and seek to eliminate or control the risks of injury and/or damage to equipment.

Operate in accordance with and comply with the Company’s Safety and Health Policy, and with all site policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.

Proactive involvement with continuous OHS improvement processes.

Active participation in all communication and awareness systems.

Actively participate in hazard reporting and other risk identification and control processes to mitigate risks in the workplace.

Follow exploration safety protocols when working around helicopters and performing helicopter-assisted fieldwork and drill operations.

Certification of bear safety shotgun course.

Environmental Requirements

Be aware of the environmental consequences associated with the tasks they perform and ensure environmental risks of tasks are assessed and mitigated prior to starting them.

Display a clear commitment to responsible environmental management.

Operate in accordance with the Company’s Environment Policy, and comply with other site environmental policies, procedures and regulatory requirements.

Manage all activities in a manner that prevents pollution and minimizes damage to the natural environment.

Minimize waste generation through reduced consumption and increased re-use and recycling.

Specific Departmental Requirements

Plan, budget, and supervise the execution of exploration programs to test targets, define or upgrade mineral resources.

Supervise and mentor Graduates, Geologists and Geological Technicians.

Supervise contractors and suppliers engaged in the exploration activities on the project area.

QAQC monitoring and validation of exploration results.

Provide timely and relevant information as requested by the supervising geologist.

Assist in the preparation of annual budgets and statutory reports.

Deliver periodic progress reports on time and in the correct format.

Liaise with Growth, Resource and Operational Geology functions, Metallurgists and Engineers.

Undertake additional responsibilities as reasonably requested by the supervisor.

Qualifications Required

Degree majoring in Geology from a recognized tertiary institution.

Minimum 3-5 years of relevant progressive experience.

Knowledge of mapping, structural geology, geochemistry, geophysics, drilling and sampling techniques. Some understanding of resource estimation methods and experience in field work.

Good communication skills.

Current Driver’s License from a U.S. State.

Desired Experience & Competencies

Able to manage a prospect scale project including, interpretation, drill design and drilling logistics.

Competent user of Leapfrog Geo, able to create a resource ready geological model.

Computer literacy – Microsoft Office, AcQuire, Reflex Hub, ArcGIS and LeapFrog Geo.

Demonstrate the ability to maintain or exceed best industry practice in all geological and environmental tasks.

Flexibility to adapt to improved procedures and practices.

First Aid certificate.

MSHA Certification

Why join us?

When you join our Company, you join the Northern Star Team. You will be engaged in a dynamic work environment where you will be valued as an integral part of the team, and where you can develop and grow with our business.

Northern Star actively supports and contributes to the local community in which we operate.
